metadata.description: JobOS.ai first-iteration: canonical Managerial + Agent job hierarchies, T-3 DSL, context graph machine, SAP pilot.

stages.name: Research
stages.description: Survey JTBD, org design, OKR/KPI, APQC taxonomies. Propose finite Managerial catalog with counts. Propose fractal Agent hierarchy schema with 10+ worked examples.

stages.name: Synthesis
stages.description: Merge research outputs into structured handoff schema. Resolve open questions. Finalize crosswalk with translation examples.

stages.name: Decision
stages.description: Human review of open_questions requiring decisions (catalog finiteness, T-3 DSL edge cases, ethics flags). Time-boxed 48h per question.

stages.name: Engineering
stages.description: Implement: T-3 DSL engine, JSON schemas, evaluation harness, APIs (hierarchy browse, agent consumption, 2D placement), SAP pilot thin-slice.

stages.name: Evaluation
stages.description: Run evaluation harness: coverage tests, orthogonality, fractal consistency, T-3 grammaticality. Publish scoring report. Merge only when thresholds met.

managerial_catalog.note: Research proposes 150 T-3 nodes (Level 3 APQC processes). T-3 entries to be populated in next engineering iteration. Each T-2 decomposes into 3-5 T-3 roadmap/blueprint jobs.

t1_programs.name: Lead Qualification Automation
t1_programs.description: Automate the qualification of inbound leads to reduce response time and improve conversion

t1_programs.name: Invoice Processing Automation
t1_programs.description: Automate matching, validation, and routing of supplier invoices

t1_programs.name: Customer Onboarding Automation
t1_programs.description: Automate user provisioning and first-experience guidance for new customers

t1_programs.name: Order-to-Cash Optimization
t1_programs.description: Optimize the end-to-end order fulfillment and revenue collection process

t1_programs.name: Procure-to-Pay Automation
t1_programs.description: Automate procurement workflows from requisition to payment
Automated market research, competitive analysis, and trend detection
Automated product roadmap analysis, feature prioritization, and lifecycle stage assessment
Demand forecasting, logistics optimization, and supplier management automation
Automated service provisioning, quality monitoring, and SLA management
Automated support routing, sentiment analysis, and retention workflows
Recruiting pipeline, skills assessment, learning path automation
Infrastructure monitoring, incident response, deployment automation
Facilities monitoring, equipment maintenance scheduling, resource allocation
Regulatory monitoring, risk assessment, audit trail automation
PR monitoring, partnership coordination, government relations tracking
Knowledge extraction, process mining, continuous improvement recommendations

agent_catalog.fractal_note: Any AJH T-2 workflow can be recursively expanded into a new T-1/T-2/T-3 hierarchy at a lower level of abstraction. This allows infinite nesting while maintaining consistent structural grammar.
t3_dsl_examples: Minimize the time to qualify a lead from first contact to CRM stage updated, measured in hours, target ≤ 4
Minimize the likelihood of invoice processing error due to PO mismatch, measured as error_rate in percent, target ≤ 0.5
Minimize the time to onboard a new user from account created to first feature used, measured in calendar_days, target ≤ 7
Minimize the likelihood of a qualified lead going uncontacted for > 1 business day, measured as miss_rate in percent, target ≤ 2
Minimize the time to provision all required software licenses from IT ticket creation to first login confirmed, measured in hours, target ≤ 4
Minimize the likelihood of SLA breach on P1 tickets, measured as breach_rate in per_1000, target ≤ 5
Minimize the time to complete financial close reconciliation from period end to CFO sign-off, measured in business_days, target ≤ 3
Minimize the likelihood of inventory count discrepancy detected at cycle audit, measured as defect_rate in percent, target ≤ 0.5
Minimize the time to verify account data from receipt to approval, measured in minutes, target ≤ 5
Minimize the likelihood of duplicate invoice entry, measured as error_rate in per_1000, target ≤ 1
Minimize the time to generate a sales quote from initial inquiry to client delivery, measured in minutes, target ≤ 15
Minimize the likelihood of data hallucination during customer sentiment analysis, measured as error_rate in per_1000, target ≤ 0.5
Minimize the time to obtain manager approval for discounts > 10% from submission to decision, measured in hours, target ≤ 2

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Define Business Concept → Market Intelligence Automation
crosswalk.notes: Agent automates market validation and concept testing subprocess

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Assess Internal Environment → Market Intelligence Automation
crosswalk.notes: Agent handles data gathering and benchmarking; strategic interpretation remains human

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Assess External Environment → Market Intelligence Automation
crosswalk.notes: Competitive intelligence and trend detection are core agent capabilities

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Develop Product Strategy → Product Lifecycle Intelligence
crosswalk.notes: Agent provides feature prioritization data; strategic decisions remain human
Agent assists with requirements analysis and design validation

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Manage Product Lifecycle → Product Lifecycle Intelligence
crosswalk.notes: Lifecycle stage tracking and transition triggers map directly
Agent handles qualification subprocess of full acquisition funnel

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Develop Marketing Strategy → Market Intelligence Automation
crosswalk.notes: Agent provides market data and campaign performance analytics
O2C spans from sales pipeline through delivery and payment

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Plan Supply Chain → Supply Chain Optimization
crosswalk.notes: Demand forecasting and supply planning are core agent capabilities
Near-direct mapping between procurement and P2P agent program
Agent handles route optimization and tracking; physical logistics remain external
Agent automates provisioning and scheduling; physical delivery remains human

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Manage Service Quality → Service Delivery Automation
crosswalk.notes: Agent monitors SLAs and flags quality deviations

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Manage Customer Support → Customer Experience Automation
crosswalk.notes: Agent handles ticket routing, FAQ responses, and escalation detection
Onboarding is a subprocess of retention strategy
Agent handles resume screening, scheduling, and initial assessment
Agent automates learning path recommendations and skills gap analysis
Agent detects attrition signals and recommends engagement actions

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Manage IT Infrastructure → IT Operations Automation
crosswalk.notes: Infrastructure monitoring and incident response are fully automatable
Agent handles data quality checks and lineage tracking; governance policy is human
Agent automates deployment, monitoring, and performance optimization

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Manage Financial Planning → Financial Planning Automation
crosswalk.notes: Agent automates forecasting models and variance analysis
Invoice processing is one subprocess within transaction processing
Agent handles cash flow forecasting; investment decisions remain human
Agent handles scheduling, monitoring, and maintenance prediction
Predictive maintenance and lifecycle tracking are automatable
Agent handles contract review and clause extraction; legal judgment is human
Regulatory monitoring and audit trail generation are core agent capabilities
Agent performs risk scoring and monitoring; risk appetite decisions remain human

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Manage Government Relations → Stakeholder Communication Automation
crosswalk.notes: Agent tracks regulatory changes and stakeholder communications
Agent handles partner performance tracking and communication scheduling

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Manage Public Relations → Stakeholder Communication Automation
crosswalk.notes: Agent monitors media, sentiment, and drafts communications

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Develop Business Capabilities → Knowledge & Improvement Automation
crosswalk.notes: Agent identifies capability gaps via process mining
Knowledge extraction and organization are core agent capabilities

crosswalk.mapping_rule: Drive Continuous Improvement → Knowledge & Improvement Automation
crosswalk.notes: Agent mines processes for improvement opportunities; implementation decisions are human

crosswalk_rules.mjh_to_ajh: MJH T-2 (What/Strategy) maps to AJH T-1 (Target/Program). MJH T-3 (How/Roadmap) maps to AJH T-2 (Workflow steps). MJH 2D Metric Axis maps to AJH T-3 (Constraints via DSL). MJH 2D Identity Axis maps to AJH metadata/style constraints.
crosswalk_rules.ajh_to_mjh: AJH T-3 metric breaches are summarized as Imperfections in MJH 2D model. Green = target met. Red = threshold exceeded, triggers human Switch decision.
crosswalk_rules.non_negotiables: Social/emotional dimensions exist ONLY in MJH. Agent hierarchy is metric-only. Social goals must be translated into Pattern B likelihood constraints.

design_decisions.title: 13 APQC-based T-1 pillars as universal finite catalog
design_decisions.decision: Use APQC PCF v8.0's 13 categories as the T-1 tier. This provides 100% coverage of business activities based on 30 years of cross-industry research.
design_decisions.rationale: Stability first — organizations should NOT create custom T-1 pillars. Use T-2/T-3 for specialization.

design_decisions.title: 2D Orthogonal Model uses CVF + KPI axes
design_decisions.decision: Dimension 1 (Identity/Culture) uses Competing Values Framework (Collaborate/Create/Compete/Control). Dimension 2 (KPI/Metric) uses domain-agnostic measurement semantics with units, targets, baselines.
design_decisions.rationale: Orthogonality prevents 'metric blindness' — AI agents pursuing efficiency must not destroy corporate culture. The 2D model is what prevents JobOS from being a mere process list.

design_decisions.title: AJH has NO emotional/social dimension
design_decisions.decision: The Agent Job Hierarchy is strictly metric-driven. Any attempt to introduce social goals leads to reasoning instability. Social goals must be translated into Pattern B 'Minimize the likelihood of...' constraints.
design_decisions.rationale: Fractal precision requires determinism. Social context is encoded as metadata/style constraints, not optimization targets.

design_decisions.title: SAP Pilot: Order-to-Cash (O2C) domain
design_decisions.decision: Use Order-to-Cash as the SAP internal pilot domain. Decompose into 5 T-2 stages: Contact→Lead, Lead→Opportunity, Opportunity→Quote, Quote→Order, Order→Cash.
design_decisions.rationale: O2C has clear metrics, high frequency, spans multiple SAP modules (SD, FI, CO), and has abundant public reference data in SAP Help Portal and Signavio.

design_decisions.title: Unified Metric ontology: KPI (continuous) + Threshold (binary)
design_decisions.decision: Metrics have two subclasses: KPI Metrics (performance, continuous scale, directionality) and Threshold Metrics (constraint, binary pass/fail, hiring criteria). Both map to T-3 DSL patterns.
design_decisions.rationale: Synthesizes ODI school (quantified outcomes) with Switch school (binary hiring/firing criteria). Both are needed for complete JTBD coverage.

design_decisions.title: AJH fractal expansion via universal T-2 step grammar
design_decisions.decision: All AJH T-2 workflows use a universal 7-step grammar: Define, Locate, Prepare, Execute, Monitor, Modify, Conclude. Any T-2 can be promoted to a root T-1 for a sub-agent.
design_decisions.rationale: Ensures structural self-similarity at every depth level. Agents parse the same grammar regardless of nesting depth.

evidence.title: JobOS.ai Canonical Schemas v1.0
evidence.notes: JSON Schema for managerial_hierarchy, agent_hierarchy, crosswalk. In src/jobos/schemas/.

evidence.title: T-3 DSL Engine
evidence.notes: Pattern A/B parser, validator, renderer, translator. 39 tests passing. In src/jobos/kernel/t3_dsl.py.

evidence.title: APQC Process Classification Framework v8.0
evidence.notes: 13-category universal business process taxonomy. 30+ years of cross-industry research. Justifies T-1 catalog count of 13.

evidence.title: Competing Values Framework (CVF)
evidence.notes: Organizational culture model: Collaborate/Create/Compete/Control quadrants. Used for Dimension 1 (Identity axis) of 2D model.

evidence.title: Gemini Blueprint Research Report
evidence.notes: Comprehensive synthesis of JTBD theory, active inference, control theory, and APQC standards. Proposes 15→13 T-1, 45→36 T-2, 150 T-3 catalog structure. Saved at materials/jobos-blueprint-research-with-jtbd-deep-dive.md

evidence.title: Gemini Project Plan & Architecture Report
evidence.notes: Full architecture proposal: dual MJH/AJH hierarchies, 2D orthogonal model, fractal execution, crosswalk ontology, evaluation criteria, pretraining strategy, SAP L2C pilot. Saved at materials/Jobos.ai-project-plan-and-Research.md

evidence.title: Ontology of Context in JTBD
evidence.notes: Formal synthesis: Circumstance vs Situation vs Condition vs Constraint. Context = spatiotemporal predicates of the struggling moment. Saved at materials/context-in-jtbd.md

evidence.title: Metrics Ontology in JTBD
evidence.notes: Unified metric specification: ODI continuous outcomes + Switch binary thresholds. Two subclasses: KPI Metrics (Performance) and Threshold Metrics (Constraint). Saved at materials/metrics-in-jtbd.md

open_questions.prompt: How many T-1 categories belong in the finite Managerial catalog? Current draft has 3. Literature (APQC PCF) suggests 12 process categories — should we align to APQC or define our own minimum set?
open_questions.decision: 13 T-1 pillars aligned directly to APQC PCF v8.0 categories. Justified by 30 years of cross-industry research providing 100% coverage. The 13 pillars should remain unchanged (0% growth) for minimum 5 years. Previous draft of 3 was insufficient — APQC provides the correct universally-validated finite set.

open_questions.prompt: What is the correct finite count for T-2 Core Functional jobs in the Managerial catalog? Current draft has 6. Should each T-1 have exactly 3–7 T-2 children?
open_questions.decision: 36 T-2 jobs (averaging 2-3 per T-1 pillar), derived from APQC Level 2 'Process Groups'. The research proposes 75-100 T-2 at full saturation but the initial catalog starts with 36 core entries. Each T-1 has 2-4 T-2 children. T-2 should see <5% annual growth.

open_questions.prompt: Should the T-3 DSL support a Pattern C for resource/cost minimization? (e.g., 'Minimize the cost of [process] from [start] to [end], measured in [currency_unit], target ≤ [N]'). This would cover financial jobs that are neither time nor likelihood.
open_questions.decision: YES — Pattern C is justified. The research identifies 'Efficiency: Cost per Task, Tokens per Task' as a canonical measure category. Financial metrics (cost-per-transaction) are pervasive in enterprise contexts. Pattern C follows: 'Minimize the cost of [process] from [start] to [end], measured in [currency_unit], target ≤ [threshold], current baseline [value].' Canonical units: USD, EUR, cost_per_transaction, tokens_per_task.

open_questions.prompt: For the SAP pilot: which SAP module and process should be the thin-slice? Candidates: (a) FI-AP invoice processing, (b) SD order-to-cash, (c) MM procurement-to-pay. Which has the clearest T-3 DSL mapping and best available public data?
open_questions.decision: Order-to-Cash (O2C) is recommended. Rationale: (1) Clear metrics and high frequency, (2) Spans multiple SAP modules (SD, FI, CO) — robust cross-pillar test, (3) Abundant public data in SAP Help Portal, Signavio, and SAP GitHub. Decompose into 5 T-2 stages: Contact→Lead, Lead→Opportunity, Opportunity→Quote, Quote→Order, Order→Cash. Source corpus: SAP Help Portal, SAP Signavio Process Insights/Explorer, answers.sap.com, SAP Build Store.

open_questions.prompt: Ethics/bias flag: When the Managerial catalog classifies jobs as 'AI-automatable', how do we ensure the catalog itself does not systematically undervalue human-only work categories (e.g., care work, creative work, relational work)?
open_questions.decision: Mitigated via three mechanisms: (1) The 2D model's Identity axis (Dimension 1) explicitly tracks cultural/social value independent of efficiency metrics — jobs that score high on Identity but low on KPI are flagged as 'Human Essential', not 'low value'. (2) The MJH T-1 pillars include 'Human Capital' and 'External Relations' which are inherently relational. (3) Guard against 'Western Management' bias by supporting diverse organizational models (non-profit, cooperative) through configurable Identity dimension weights. The taxonomy classifies 'what needs to be done' not 'what can be automated.'

acceptance_criteria.text: Managerial catalog proposes T-1, T-2, T-3 counts with definitions and 2D placement for each entry
Agent hierarchy schema includes 10+ worked T-3 DSL examples across ≥ 3 industries
All T-3 DSL examples parse and validate against the T-3 DSL engine without errors
Crosswalk covers ≥ 80% of Managerial T-2 nodes with agent counterparts
T-3 DSL parser handles Pattern A and Pattern B with ≤, <=, and < operators
JSON schemas define all tier properties, provenance, and versioning fields
API endpoints for T-3 validate, parse, render, translate, and 2D classify
Evaluation harness runs: coverage, orthogonality, fractal consistency, T-3 grammaticality. All ≥ 95% pass rate
T-3 DSL supports Pattern C (cost/resource minimization)
Managerial catalog expanded to 13 T-1 pillars and 36 T-2 entries in Neo4j schema
SAP O2C pilot: 5 T-2 stages with T-3 constraints defined and parseable

engineering_tasks.title: Agent hierarchy JSON Schema with fractal structure
Crosswalk JSON Schema with 12 worked examples
Schema API endpoints (validate, parse, render, translate, classify-2d)
Evaluation harness (68 tests for DSL + schemas)
SAP O2C pilot: ingest 5 T-2 stages with T-3 constraints
Expand T-1 catalog to 13 APQC pillars in Neo4j + seed data
Expand T-2 catalog to 36 entries with parent_t1_id links
Implement Pattern C (cost minimization) in T-3 DSL
2D orthogonal scoring with CVF Identity axis
Crosswalk transform: MJH T-2 → AJH T-1 automated mapping
Pretraining data pipeline: dedup + PII filter + domain balance

tests.name: dsl_grammar_check
tests.description: All T-3 DSL examples in spec parse without error (Pattern A, B, and C)

tests.name: crosswalk_roundtrip
tests.description: Every managerial T-2 has at least one agent DSL counterpart or explicit no_equivalent

tests.name: orthogonality_check
tests.description: No T-3 statement contains technology/product names (solution-agnostic)

tests.name: fractal_consistency
tests.description: Agent hierarchy nodes at depth 1-5 all parse to valid T-3 constraints

tests.name: schema_unit_tests
tests.description: All schema + DSL unit tests pass

tests.name: t3_coverage
tests.description: Worked examples cover all TimeUnit and RateType enum values

tests.name: inter_rater_reliability
tests.description: Classification audit: Cohen's kappa ≥ 0.85 when mapping random process descriptions to T-1/T-2

tests.name: cross_domain_coverage
tests.description: ≥ 98% of SAP O2C process steps map to a T-3 job without Miscellaneous category
